results.snippet: Nexus Mods' production redesign of their mod browsing experience, covering exactly the problem space of 291-card grids. Key UX decisions: they introduced a tile-first layout but faced intense community backlash (280k views, 2k comments, mostly negative) because it reduced visible card density. The team subsequently committed to adding a compact list view and additional tile density options. Directly applicable: the tension between visual card richness and information density is the central design problem for any mod browser at this scale.
results.title: An Update on the Mod Browsing Overhaul — Nexus Mods

results.snippet: Community critique thread on the Nexus Mods redesign with thousands of practitioner-level comments. Surfaced the core complaint: large image-heavy cards that look good in mockups reduce browsable density for power users who scan 291+ mods by name/category. The consensus leans toward compact list view as default with optional tile toggle — directly informing card density toggle UX for a CoH mod browser.
results.title: Mod Browsing Overhaul Discussion — Nexus Mods Forums

results.snippet: Open GitHub issue on Modrinth (fully open-source mod platform, MIT-licensed) requesting a tile/grid/list view toggle — the same feature needed for a CoH mod browser. Modrinth's codebase at github.com/modrinth/code is inspectable Vue/Nuxt source showing their actual CSS grid implementation for mod cards with name, category, download count, and description fields. The issue thread documents which CurseForge UX patterns users want replicated.
results.title: Tiles or grid feature to view mods and library (CurseForge feature) · Issue #2850 · modrinth/code

results.snippet: Authoritative reference for native CSS masonry via grid-template-rows: masonry (shipping in Firefox, Safari TP, Chrome behind flags as of mid-2026). Covers the progressive-enhancement pattern — base grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax()) gives every browser a working layout, and @supports layers masonry on top. Featured/hero cards spanning columns work via standard grid-column: span 2 on the defined axis. Critical for a 291-card CoH browser needing variable-height description text.
results.title: Masonry layout — CSS Grid Layout (MDN Web Docs)

results.snippet: Smashing Magazine's deep-dive on production-ready CSS masonry covering the @supports progressive-enhancement approach, multi-column span for hero/featured cards, and fallback behavior. Includes worked examples of card grids that match the CoH mod browser's anatomy (image thumb, title, meta badges, description, CTA button). Useful for implementing the featured-mod hero card that spans 2 columns without JavaScript.
results.title: Native CSS Masonry Layout In CSS Grid — Smashing Magazine

results.snippet: Red Hat's open-source PatternFly design system documents the card-view-to-list-view toggle pattern with explicit UX rationale: card view is for gallery/browsing mode, list/table view is for comparison and bulk action. Their card anatomy (header badge, body description, footer action) maps closely to the CoH mod card's name + category badge + download count + description + download button structure. Provides coded React examples with density toggle accessible from a toolbar.
results.title: PatternFly Card View Design Guidelines
